notes: Solid, well-structured CLI tool. SECURITY: Good XSS prevention with escapeHtml/escapeAttr covering all 5 HTML entities on all user-controlled values in HTML output. baseUrl validated for http(s) scheme. However: (1) absolute filePath leaked in JSON search index = information disclosure, (2) no symlink-aware path traversal guard — discovered files could escape inputDir via symlinks, (3) no input length limit on CLI args/env vars themselves. ERRHANDLING: Custom error hierarchy (ConfigError/ParseError/IOError) is well-designed. Per-file errors caught gracefully without aborting run. Global uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers present. Config validates all errors before failing. BUT line 471 has a real bug: `new Date(invalidString).toISOString()` throws RangeError instead of falling through to the `||` fallback — the `updatedAt` fallback is dead code for invalid dates, meaning a malformed 'updated' frontmatter field will crash processing for that file (caught by outer try/catch, so not fatal, but unintended). BESTPRACTICES: 'use strict', shebang, structured JSON logging with severity-appropriate streams (stderr for WARN+), metrics tracking, stable sort ordering, BOM handling, --help flag, env+CLI config layering, ensureDir before writes. Missing: no TypeScript/JSDoc type annotations beyond one docblock, no test suite. ROBUSTNESS: Handles many edge cases (BOM, missing frontmatter, empty dirs, oversized files, duplicate slugs, draft filtering). YAML parser is intentionally minimal but misses multiline values, nested objects, and colons in values. Line 278 `rawValue.trim() === null` is dead code (trimmed string never null). No circular symlink protection in recursive walk — could stack overflow. bodySnippet stripping is minimal (only headers and bold). Synchronous I/O is fine for CLI but no concurrency option.
